channel.title: Bloomberg Green Global
channel.description: Últimos artigos de Bloomberg Green Global

item.title: CME Plans to Launch Wind Derivatives for US, Europe, Australia
item.description: The Chicago Mercantile Exchange plans to launch wind derivatives across three continents, according to a person familiar with the matter, as the role of renewable energy in power generation grows.
item.dc:creator: Mary Hui, Joe Wertz

item.title: Heat Wave Breaks French and UK Records as Temperatures Soar
item.description: The heat wave baking western Europe smashed records in the UK and France, as the region faces another day of disruptive temperatures.
item.dc:creator: Joe Wertz, Nayla Razzouk

item.title: Top Climate Fund Says 75 Countries Apply for Adaptation Finance
item.description: The Climate Investment Funds, a $14 billion pool of climate-focused capital, said 75 countries applied for adaptation finance through a new program designed to promote initiatives aimed at providing protection against the impact of climate warming.
item.dc:creator: Antony Sguazzin

item.title: US Solar Is Thriving in the Face of Trump’s Tariffs
item.description: The American administration is dismantling clean-energy policies, yet 90% of all new power-generating capacity added to the grid last year was made up of solar, wind and batteries.
item.dc:creator: Akshat Rathi, Oscar Boyd

item.title: India’s $55 Billion Green Energy Pipeline Faces Climate Damage
item.description: The majority of India’s planned renewable energy infrastructure will be exposed to escalating climate hazards, putting about $55 billion of physical assets at risk of damage by the end of this decade, according to a new study.
item.dc:creator: Azman Usmani

item.title: Guangzhou Exchange Considering Potential of Cobalt Metal Futures
item.description: Guangzhou Futures Exchange is considering launching China’s first futures for cobalt, a metal used in batteries as well as the aerospace and defense industries, according to people familiar with the matter.
item.dc:creator: Annie Lee

item.title: US Warns That Gas Will Go Outside EU if Bloc Keeps Methane Rules
item.description: US Energy Secretary Chris Wright warned the European Union that US gas supplies will head elsewhere if the bloc refuses to ease regulations to tackle methane, a potent greenhouse gas.
item.dc:creator: John Ainger

item.title: US Largest Grid Updates Emergency Plan as AI Stretches Capacity
item.description: The largest US power grid is creating a new emergency warning as surging data-center demand pushes electricity supplies toward shortages beyond periods of extreme weather.
item.dc:creator: John Ainger

item.title: France Records Hottest Day as European Heat Wave Intensifies
item.description: France recorded its hottest ever night and day as the heat wave searing western Europe deepened, disrupting schools, transport and tourist sites.
item.dc:creator: Joe Wertz, Nayla Razzouk

item.title: Modern Homes Leave Londoners Struggling to Cope With Extreme Heat
item.description: The British capital is baking as a heat wave sweeps through Europe.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Rudgard, Akshat Rathi, Alastair Marsh
